VQE_DroneScrapyard.questDescriptionRules.li:
questDescription->[asker_nameDef], [asker_faction_leaderTitle] of [asker_faction_name], has sent the coordinates of a den of discarded military drones. Once built for war, these machines now gather among heaps of scrap, struggling to fulfill directives that no longer serve any purpose. Some drag themselves forward aimlessly, while others are observed to emit distorted whines or engage in crude, half-functional cooperation.

[asker_faction_name] plans to chart the site, and needs the hostile drones deactivated. They will reward us for making sure the area is clear of the unpredictable threats.

At the center of the scrapyard lies a fragmented chunk of a military command drone, which can be scavenged for vital intelligence on proprietary drone prototypes. This data can be used by the colony to manufacture drone types once thought lost to time.

Defending drones:
[defendingDrones]

questDescription->[asker_nameDef], [asker_faction_leaderTitle] of [asker_faction_name], has transmitted the location of a vast, rusted scrapyard filled with decommissioned war drones. Left to rot among mountains of jagged metal, these machines attempt futile maintenance protocols, welding incompatible scrap to their own chassis in a desperate bid to remain functional.

[asker_faction_name] seeks to harvest the rare plasteel and components buried in the heaps, but the erratic, territorial machines make salvage operations impossible. They will pay us well to clear the junk-strewn field.

Buried deep within the central scrap pile is an uncorrupted memory matrix from a destroyed logistics mech. Extracting this data cache will grant the colony crucial insights into high-efficiency power storage technologies.

Defending drones:
[defendingDrones]

questDescription->[asker_nameDef], [asker_faction_leaderTitle] of [asker_faction_name], has pointed us toward a desolate canyon serving as a massive dumping ground for obsolete mechanized units. Half-buried in slag and debris, these forgotten drones twitch and spark, hopelessly trying to recharge from depleted power cells while lashing out at any movement.

[asker_faction_name] intends to establish a new forward outpost in this highly defensible canyon, but they need the unpredictable scrap-guardians eliminated first. A handsome reward is offered for securing the area.

Resting atop the largest pile of refuse is an intact targeting sensor from a decommissioned sniper drone. Salvaging this pristine hardware will allow the colony to reverse-engineer advanced long-range optical arrays.

Defending drones:
[defendingDrones]

questDescription->[asker_nameDef], [asker_faction_leaderTitle] of [asker_faction_name], has shared coordinates for a sprawling technological wasteland, choked with the rusted husks of military drones. Many of the machines have reactivated due to short circuits, aimlessly patrolling the junk hills and occasionally discharging their weapons at falling debris.

[asker_faction_name] claims these unpredictable junkers keep ambushing their merchant caravans passing through the sector. They have offered a bounty to anyone willing to permanently deactivate the threat.

Beneath a collapsed tower of scrap metal lies a heavy siege drone's sealed black box. Breaking it open will yield classified encryption keys, significantly advancing our understanding of mechanoid signal security.

Defending drones:
[defendingDrones]

questDescription->[asker_nameDef], [asker_faction_leaderTitle] of [asker_faction_name], has sent the location of a heavily polluted drone graveyard. Centuries of accumulated metallic refuse have created a maze of junk, patrolled by mangled warframes that drag their missing limbs through the dirt, still violently executing forgotten guard directives.

[asker_faction_name] is attempting an archaeological excavation of the ancient ruins buried beneath the scrap, but the aggressive machines are halting progress. They will compensate us generously for purging the site.

Hidden in the heart of the dumping ground is a heavily armored processing core from an old command unit. Securing this core will provide our researchers with the schematics necessary to manufacture reinforced mechanoid plating.

Defending drones:
[defendingDrones]

questDescription->[asker_nameDef], [asker_faction_leaderTitle] of [asker_faction_name], has uncovered a dense field of mechanical refuse, where thousands of discarded drones have been laid to rest. However, a corrupted distress beacon has awakened a handful of them. Now, these broken machines shamble aimlessly through the scrap, their audio sensors emitting sorrowful, static-laced warning chimes.

[asker_faction_name] is experiencing severe communications jamming due to the erratic signals broadcast by the restless machines, and needs the scrapyard silenced. They are willing to pay a premium for our help.

At the epicenter of the electronic noise is an ancient, hyper-dense communications relay. Scavenging this part will provide the colony with invaluable data on optimizing automated bandwidth and mechanoid control frequencies.

Defending drones:
[defendingDrones]
